title: Integrated Master’s Program
content:
Integrated Master’s Program

The Integrated Master’s Program is an academic pathway designed to allow high-achieving undergraduate students, whose cumulative GPA is above a specified level to take master’s-level courses while they are still completing their bachelor’s degree. The program also aims to help students gain practical professional experience, plan their graduate studies before graduating, and encourage them to pursue a master’s degree.

Undergraduate students with a cumulative GPA of 3.00/4.00 or higher, who have successfully completed all courses taken in previous semesters, may apply for the Integrated Master’s Program designated for their enrolled undergraduate program starting from the beginning of the seventh semester. Applications are evaluated provided that the additional requirements set out in the Üsküdar University Integrated Master’s Program Directive are also met.

While continuing their undergraduate education, students will take at least one and at most two graduate-level courses each semester. Students are required to complete their undergraduate program within eight semesters. If final enrollment procedures are completed after graduation, the courses taken and successfully completed within the scope of the Integrated Master’s Program will be transferred and credited toward the graduate program.

For more detailed information, please consult the

Integrated Master’s Program Directive.
